stem: In a collection, 25% of the cards are foreign. Of all the foreign cards in the collection, 60% are rare. What percentage of the cards in the collection are foreign and rare?
rationale: Choice B is correct. The percentage of cards that are both foreign and rare equals the percentage that are foreign times the percentage of those that are rare: 25% × 60% = 0.25 × 0.60 = 0.15 = 15%. Choice A is incorrect because 10% would result from subtracting (25% - 15%) or some other miscombination. Choice C is incorrect because 35% would result from adding 25% + 10% instead of multiplying. Choice D is incorrect because 85% has no meaningful relation to the given percentages and likely results from subtracting 15% from 100%.
